Get Started — Internal

Changelog Guidelines

Standards for creating and structuring Changelogs across change request development cycles.

Definition and Objective

Definition

A changelog is an informal document of a Change Request (requirement). It is an in-between step between a client's requirements (in any form such as email, chats, or documents) and a Business Requirements Document (BRD).

Objective

BRDs' detail-oriented nature requires time to complete. This requirement stalls development in two ways:

  • Pausing development until a BRD is finalized creates a delay
  • Changes in requirements during development do not end up being reflected in the BRD

Since Changelogs are easy to create, they solve both problems. Additionally, the organization of a Changelog makes it:

  • Easier for anyone to write
  • Easier for developers and designers to understand and implement

Format

The name of a Changelog should be categorized into one of two types. Each type uses a specific format to ensure consistent monitoring and traceability.

Standard

Standard

Used to track and document requested changes systematically. Must clearly indicate the version and status to ensure proper monitoring and traceability.

Version Number
Identifies the version of the requested changes.
Status
Represents the current progress of the request.
INC TBD ACTIVE DONE
CR Title
A concise title summarizing the requested change.
Format [Status] [Version (Optional)] – [Title]
Examples
ACTIVE v3.1 – Voyadores Incident Report DONE – Voyadores Payroll Enhancement TBD – Fleet Management Route Optimization
Customization

Customization

Used to track customization requests for a specific account. Must clearly indicate the status, version, account code, and change request title to ensure accurate documentation and traceability.

Version Number
Specifies the version of the requested changes.
Status
Indicates the current progress of the update.
INC TBD ACTIVE DONE
Account Code
Identifies the specific account associated with the customization request.
CR Title
A concise title summarizing the requested change.
Format [Status] [Version (Optional)] – [Account Code]: [Title]
Examples
ACTIVE v2.0 – NESV001: HR Payroll Customization DONE – VYD002: Inventory Module Enhancement

Body

1

Main Software Requirement

Describe the main software requirement specification in a sentence. Provide a short but concise description containing the main objective and general behavior of the change.

2

Breakdown

Specify the breakdown of the main requirement in the Changelog body. It should be bulleted with detailed instruction to group changes respectively and quantify them. Each item should be organized by change type:

Visible Change

Changes that are perceptible to the user — UI modifications, new screens, updated interactions.

Structure
▸ Module
▸ Screen
• UI element (modals, views, section) – description of the change
• Feature description with scenarios

It is important to lay down the hierarchical location of the change. This helps localize the changes in the application. Typically follows: Module › Sub-module › Screen › UI Element. However, this is a guide — change will vary from feature to feature.

Non-visible Change

Changes that are not directly perceptible to the user — backend logic, data handling, performance improvements.

Structure
▸ Module
• Description of the change

Non-visible changes have a laxer structure. Typically, it involves identifying the topmost hierarchical group and specifying the changes under it.

Status Reference

INC Incomplete The changelog is still being written and is not yet ready for review or development.
TBD To Be Determined Requirements are pending clarification or approval before development can begin.
ACTIVE Active The changelog has been finalized and development is currently in progress.
DONE Done All changes in this changelog have been implemented and completed.
On this page
